channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquake Death Toll Nears 2,000
item.description: The death toll from the twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela last week rose to nearly 2,000, as aftershocks eased in both frequency and intensity and authorities expanded recovery efforts. Almost 20,000 people survived the disaster in the hardest-hit coastal state of La Guaira, National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez said Tuesday during a televised briefing. The earthquakes left more than 10,000 people injured and 15,000 displaced, he added.

item.title: Wayve Courts Automakers With AI Driving System That Learns Like Humans
item.description: Wayve uses an artificial-intelligence technology called end-to-end machine learning to navigate roadways, which is supposed to instantly translate sensor-generated data into driving decisions, much like a human driver. Wayve engineers believe the conventional, programming-intensive safety approach hinders an AI driving system’s ability to stay safe in unusual cases because it is hard to write rules to prepare for very unusual situations. Autonomous-driving startup Wayve is riding a tide of investor interest.

item.title: July 4 Holiday Cargo Thieves Are Ready Now, Report Warns
item.description: Cargo thieves may be operating sooner than you think to take advantage of the July 4 holiday, a new report shows. Verisk CargoNet is warning transportation and logistics stakeholders to prepare for elevated cargo theft risk during the July 4 holiday, when reduced staffing, closures and staged freight can create opportunities for organized crime. The predictable disruption in the supply chain leads Cargo thieves to understand when freight is likely to be parked, when facilities may be closed and when normal verification procedures may be loser, the report’s authors say.

item.title: Recreational Marijuana Can Be Sold in Virginia Retail Stores Beginning in 2027
item.description: “This compromise gives us a smarter and safer path forward — one that protects consumers, keeps products tested and accurately labeled, and creates a legal marketplace that is affordable and accessible enough to actually compete.” Here’s what to know about Virginia’s new law, the long process of enabling retail sales and how the state’s changes fit into the national picture: Virginia’s law allows new stores, higher possession limit Virginia already had a medical marijuana program that allowed patients to purchase the drug through dispensaries. Five years after becoming the first Southern state to legalize possession of marijuana, Virginia has approved a legal way to sell it to recreational users. State budget legislation enacted Monday will allow up to 350 cannabis shops to open across Virginia beginning July 1, 2027.

item.title: NY Man Sentenced for Fencing Organized Crime Ring’s $4.4M Jewelry Theft
item.description: According to court documents and statements made in court, between May 2023 and April 2024, an organized jewelry theft ring of Colombian nationals burglarized jewelry stores and kiosks in malls in Connecticut, Illinois, Indiana, New Jersey, Ohio, and Virginia. A Brooklyn, New York, man was sentenced last week for fencing stolen jewelry taken from stores and kiosks across the country, according to the United States Attorney’s Office for the District of Columbia. The man, sentenced to serve 24 months in prison and three years of supervised release, knowingly bought and sold jewelry stolen from mall-based stores and kiosks across the country.

item.title: Ford Recall Covers 741,000 U.S. Vehicles Due to Potential Park System Defect
item.description: over a transmission defect that can damage the park system, which in turn could result in vehicles rolling away, the U.S. Ford is recalling 741,195 vehicles in the U.S. The recall affects certain 2018-2021 Navigator, Expedition, 2020-2021 Explorer, Lincoln Aviator, and 2021 F-150 vehicles, the regulator said.

item.title: Record reinsurance capital drives creative solutions at mid-year renewals: Aon
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Alternative / ILS capital rises to $141bn, drives reinsurance market growth in early 2026: Aon
item.description: Reinsurance capital reached a record high of $790 billion by the end of Q1 2026, but it was alternative or third-party reinsurance capital, so that deployed through insurance-linked securities and related collateralized structures, which was the sole driver of growth as it reached $141 billion, rising 4% from the end of the year, according to Aon. “A stable, well-capitalized and competitive reinsurance market provides insurers with an opportunity to align capital more closely with their risk strategies while using analytics and insight to support long-term growth,” explained George Attard, chief strategy officer, Reinsurance, Aon. Now, an additional 4% of growth was seen in the first-quarter of 2026, taking the total ILS capital in the reinsurance sector to $141 billion, a new high by Aon’s numbers.

item.title: Despite reduced expected returns, ILS investor appetite has not retreated: Wakefield, Gallagher Re
item.description: Commenting on the cat bond market, Wakefield added, “Catastrophe bond issuance has run at or ahead of last year’s record pace and spreads have compressed by roughly a fifth, yet investor appetite has not retreated as expected returns have reduced.” He added that, “ILS and traditional markets are increasingly comparable, providing greater flexibility for cedants and the ability to optimize their spend across both.” Capital levels in reinsurance continue to outpace new demand for protection, which means certain parts of the market are being pressured by the weight of inflows. Even as spreads have compressed by roughly one-fifth and expected returns have fallen, Tom Wakefield, CEO of reinsurance broker Gallagher Re has said there is no sign of investor appetite retreating at this time. While catastrophe bonds remain a significant driver of rising capital levels in reinsurance, for property catastrophe risks, Wakefield highlighted expansion of the ILS market into other lines of business.

item.title: Primary insurers to bear the brunt of Q2’26 loss events: Goldman Sachs
item.description: Goldman Sachs commented, “We expect primary insurers will bear the brunt of this quarter’s loss events, as the size of each event is not overly material, and SCS losses are historically retained by primary insurers.” The firm noted that the most material natural catastrophe losses are expected to stem from a series of severe weather events, based on insured loss estimates from Gallagher Re. New analysis from Goldman Sachs on Q2 2026 catastrophe events, drawing on data from insurance brokers and catastrophe modelling firms, estimates global insured catastrophe losses at approximately $24 billion, with primary insurers expected to absorb the majority of the impact. Goldman Sachs highlighted that the earthquake in the Philippines is likely to be one of the largest insured natural catastrophe events outside the U.S., while damage from the Venezuela earthquake is expected to have a limited impact on U.S.

item.title: VIG Re obtains regulatory approval for APAC Representative Office in Singapore
item.description: VIG Re, the reinsurance arm of Vienna Insurance Group, has received the required regulatory approval from the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) and fulfilled applicable requirements to establish its Representative Office in Singapore. As previously reported, the office will be led by Wilfrid Goh, who has been appointed Chief Representative Asia Pacific (APAC). The Singapore office will serve as a liaison and coordination point between VIG Re’s headquarters and its regional stakeholders.

item.title: Flood Re sets out reform programme to update flood insurance ahead of next decade
item.description: Flood Re, the UK government-supported reinsurance scheme created to improve access to affordable flood insurance for households at higher risk, has set out a series of reforms to mark its tenth year of operation. The organisation says the changes are intended to keep insurance affordable and workable for the long term while placing greater weight on reducing flood risk and improving resilience across homes in the UK. Since its launch in 2016, Flood Re states that it has supported more than 742,000 households in accessing affordable flood insurance.

item.title: St. Pete Mayor Accepts $275M Bid to Redevelop Tropicana Field Area for Housing
item.description: Petersburg, Florida, has accepted a $275 million bid to redevelop the Gas Plant District after the Tampa Bay Rays Major League Baseball team leaves Tropicana Field for new digs in Tampa. Tropicana Field, the home of the Rays since 1990, was heavily damaged by Hurricane Milton in 2024. Photo: Tropicana Field and surrounding Gas Plant District in St.

item.title: Markets/Coverages: Zurich Launches Life Sciences Insurance Offering in Europe
item.description: Zurich Insurance Group is launching a dedicated life science insurance offering from July 2026, expanding its established U.S. As part of the offering, Zurich Resilience Solutions provides targeted consultancy and risk engineering support to help life sciences companies strengthen their operational resilience and risk management. “Life sciences companies operate in an environment of rapid innovation, regulatory complexity and evolving risks as they scale,” said Luciano Ciriná, CEO Commercial Insurance International, in a statement.

item.title: People Moves: Everest Global Wholesale & Specialty Names 2 Key Leaders, Buonpane and Stewart; TMK Appoints Barnes as Departmental Head of Marine & Energy
item.description: Everest Global Wholesale & Specialty Names 2 Key Leaders, Buonpane and Stewart Everest Group Ltd ., the Bermuda-based re/insurer, announced two key leadership appointments within Everest Global Wholesale & Specialty (GWS), effective October 1. Stephen Buonpane has been appointed global head of Construction, GWS, reporting to Jason Keen , executive vice president and chief executive officer of Everest Global Wholesale & Specialty. Additionally, Danielle Stewart has been promoted as president of Everest Evolution (Evolution), reporting to Lisa Davis , head of North America, Wholesale & Specialty.

item.title: Acrisure Launches Asero Insurance Services to Unite US Specialty MGAs Under One Brand
item.description: The post Acrisure Launches Asero Insurance Services to Unite US Specialty MGAs Under One Brand appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Acrisure has launched Asero Insurance Services , a new specialty managing general agency (MGA) platform that brings together several of its US programme administrators under a single brand as the global fintech company continues to expand its specialty underwriting operations. At Asero, underwriting comes first.” Continuing Specialty Expansion The launch of Asero follows Acrisure’s recent introduction of  Ascendri Insurance Services  and its acquisition of property MGA  Vave , underscoring the company’s strategy of expanding its specialty underwriting capabilities through both acquisitions and platform integration.

item.title: July 2026 ITL FOCUS: Cyber
item.description: ITL FOCUS is a monthly initiative featuring topics related to innovation in risk management and insurance. The first known cyber insurance policy was issued in 1997 through AIG — a $15 million coverage limit for internet-related risks at a time when many executives still weren't sure the internet would last. Nearly three decades later, cyber is one of the fastest-moving, most competitive lines in all of insurance.

item.title: Tesla files new auto insurance program in Washington
item.description: Tesla General Insurance has submitted a new private auto insurance program in Washington. The program will have two safety scores – Safety Score and Safety Score with full self-driving (supervised). Tesla is currently offering coverage in 15 states.

item.title: Nexar and Nauto to merge
item.description: Nexar and Nauto have entered into a definitive agreement to merge. Following the merger, Nexar CEO Zach Greenberger will lead the combined company, while Nauto founder and CEO Stefan Heck will serve as chairman of the board. Founded in 2015, Nexar has raised $149.5 million and has a team of about 141 employees, according to LinkedIn.

item.title: Higginbotham expands in Georgia with Blanton & Griffin partnership
item.description: Higginbotham has partnered with Blanton & Griffin Insurance Agency , an independent property and casualty agency based in Valdosta, Georgia, expanding its presence in South Georgia. Under the partnership, Blanton & Griffin will gain access to Higginbotham’s carrier relationships, specialty programs, captive capabilities, in-house claims advocacy and legal resources, and employee benefits offerings, while continuing to operate locally. Founded through a 1985 merger and with roots dating back to 1920, Blanton & Griffin serves commercial and personal lines clients, with expertise in construction, hospitality, manufacturing, nonprofits, and contract surety.

item.title: Hippo Expands Accelerant Relationship
item.description: Hippo says the expanded relationship with Accelerant, a data-driven risk exchange platform for the specialty insurance market, supports its strategy of growing a diversified insurance portfolio through disciplined underwriting and continuous optimization. (San Jose, Calif.) has expanded its relationship with Accelerant to serve as a fronting carrier for more than $500 million in annual gross written premium across Accelerant’s U.S. Hippo says the agreement gives it access to Accelerant’s specialty insurance portfolio, risk-selection tools and analytics, expanded distribution, and reinsurance connectivity to Accelerant’s Risk Capital Partners.

item.title: Non-life insurance-linked securities capital hit record $142bn after Q1: Gallagher Securities
item.description: The amount of non-life alternative reinsurance capital from catastrophe bonds and insurance-linked securities (ILS) grew by more than 5% just in the first-quarter of the year, with Gallagher Securities estimating $7 billion of incremental capital was added, to take non-life ILS capital outstanding to $142 billion at the end of Q1 2026. Now, the capital markets and insurance-linked securities specialist investment banking arm of the broker, Gallagher Securities, has explained that the growth trajectory continued in early 2026. In the latest reinsurance renewals report from the broker, the Gallagher Securities team highlight this impressive growth and explain that non-life ILS capital outstanding grew by $7 billion in the period.

item.title: Cambridge underscores its ILS advisory capability, sees asset class as valuable diversifier
item.description: Cambridge Associates, the global investment firm that provides specialist advisory and portfolio construction services, highlights insurance-linked securities, seeing the asset class as a valuable diversifier for client portfolios. Cambridge Associates has formalised a specialist ILS focused advisory team and is adding one new member to support its experts as they encounter growing interest from clients in the asset class. “In that context, ILS may continue to play an important role, and our continued investment in a specialised advisory and research team reflects its ongoing focus on the asset class.” “Cambridge Associates’s investment in its ILS team should be seen as a sign of its commitment to the asset class and maintaining our strong research quality,” Knecht explained.

item.title: Kingstone grows catastrophe reinsurance limit again, adds wildfire cover, lowers cost 15%+
item.description: Kingstone Companies, the New York and northeast US focused insurance group, has grown its catastrophe reinsurance limit again to secure a $500 million tower for 2026, while adding wildfire coverage for the first time and reporting that the risk-adjusted cost fell by more than 15%. In securing a further 14% increase in catastrophe reinsurance limit for 2026, with the $500 million tower, Kingstone Companies has kept low first-event retentions of $3.5 million for the new peril addition of wildfire, $5 million for named storm and $6 million for winter storm. “While we raised the limit purchased, added wildfire to the mix and improved terms, the cost of our core catastrophe excess of loss coverage decreased more than 15% on a risk-adjusted basis.

item.title: Kingstone lifts cat XoL reinsurance limit to $500m with 15%+ cost reduction
item.description: Kingstone Companies, Inc., a regional property and casualty insurance holding company, managed to cut the cost of its core catastrophe excess of loss (XoL) reinsurance coverage by over 15% for 2026/2027, despite purchasing more limit, improving terms, and adding the wildfire peril to its programme. Mid-year reinsurance renewal reports and commentary from broking groups have highlighted continued catastrophe reinsurance softening , and Kingstone is the latest buyer to report a significant risk-adjusted cost decrease for its 2026-2027 renewal. Kingstone’s 2026/2027 core catastrophe reinsurance programme is effective from July 1st, 2026, through June 30th, 2027, and provides the firm with $500 million of loss limit.

item.title: Dominican Republic integrates parametric insurance into adaptive social protection system
item.description: The Dominican Republic has become the first country in Latin America and the Caribbean to integrate parametric insurance into its adaptive social protection system. By building insurance into the Supérate social protection programme, the Dominican Republic has combined innovation, financial protection and social inclusion to help vulnerable households recover faster from climate shocks. The Dominican Republic is demonstrating how partnerships between governments, development institutions and the private sector can build these systems and strengthen resilience where it matters most — among vulnerable households.” Ivo Menzinger, Chair of the IDF Operating Committee, said, “This step by the Dominican Republic demonstrates how countries can harness the capabilities of the insurance industry to strengthen preparedness ahead of disasters – protecting lives, safeguarding fiscal stability, and supporting long-term development objectives.

item.title: Record $790bn reinsurance capital underpins softer mid-year renewals: Aon
item.description: Global professional services firm Aon has revealed that insurers secured double-digit pricing reductions and improved terms and conditions on their property catastrophe reinsurance placements during the June 1 and July 1, 2026, renewals, as record levels of reinsurance capital created greater flexibility for cedants seeking more tailored reinsurance solutions. According to Aon’s Reinsurance Market Dynamics Midyear 2026 Renewal Report, global reinsurance capital reached a record $790 billion as of March 31, 2026, driven primarily by continued growth in alternative capital, a trend that we and our sister publication, Artemis, have covered extensively. The firm’s report continued, “The midyear renewals also demonstrated a continued shift towards more customised and creative reinsurance solutions.

item.title: Iran Says Ship Ran Aground in ‘Unauthorized’ Hormuz Transit
item.description: Iran said a foreign container ship ran aground while sailing through the Strait of Hormuz via an “unauthorized” route, repeating its warning that vessels should only transit the waterway in coordination with the Islamic Republic. Iran has sought to control shipping traffic through the waterway since the start of the war with the US and Israel in late February, demanding fees from ships seeking to transit and ordering them to use a route that’s closer to its shore. “The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy has repeatedly warned ship captains, owners and officials of global shipping companies that any entry or exit via routes other than the authorized one in the Persian Gulf could lead to irreparable consequences,” the report said.

item.title: The Fidelis Partnership Launches US SME Casualty Reinsurance MGA
item.description: Pine Walk, the specialist MGA platform of The Fidelis Partnership (TFP), announced the launch of Adroit Underwriting Ltd., a Bermuda-based managing general agent that is focused on US small-and-medium enterprise (SME) casualty reinsurance. Adroit aims to provide disciplined, data-driven reinsurance solutions across a broad suite of US casualty lines, including medical malpractice, cyber liability, general liability, construction (architects & engineers), public entity, D&O/E&O, and lawyers legal liability. Markowski is joined by Derrick Holmes as chief financial officer and chief operating officer, who brings over a decade of reinsurance finance and operational experience, including responsibility for agent operations with Peak Capital Ltd., as well as significant ILS market experience to support a growing investor appetite in casualty risk.

item.title: People Moves: AIG Hires Bewlay From AXA Group to Become Global CUO
item.description: Nancy Bewlay will become American International Group’s new global chief underwriting officer in the insurer’s latest development at top leadership. Bewlay, who was most recently group chief underwriting and pricing officer at AXA Group since 2023, will start her new role with AIG on Sept. Bewlay will take over for Christopher Flatt, who was moved from head of AIG North America alternative channels to the role of global CUO at the start of 2025.

item.title: QBE and Claims Partners Launch UK Insurance Sector’s First Collective Rewilding Initiative
item.description: The post QBE and Claims Partners Launch UK Insurance Sector’s First Collective Rewilding Initiative appeared first on Insurtech Insights . QBE has partnered with seven organisations across its UK claims supply chain to launch what is believed to be the insurance sector’s first collective rewilding consortium, committing to a 50-year investment in nature restoration and carbon removal. The initiative, known as the QBE Buyers Club , brings together insurers, law firms, claims specialists and service providers to co-invest in the restoration of Boothby Wildland in Lincolnshire, reflecting the insurance industry’s growing focus on climate resilience and environmental sustainability.

item.title: Hippo Expands Accelerant Partnership to Grow Specialty Insurance Business
item.description: Hippo Holdings has expanded its partnership with specialty insurance platform Accelerant, a move that will significantly increase its presence in the U.S. Expanding Specialty Market Presence The partnership builds on the relationship established between Hippo and Accelerant in 2025 and reflects Hippo’s strategy of diversifying its underwriting portfolio while expanding into specialty insurance. Through Accelerant’s Risk Exchange platform, Hippo will gain access to a broader specialty insurance portfolio, advanced risk selection capabilities, expanded distribution channels and enhanced reinsurance connectivity through Accelerant’s Risk Capital Partners.

item.title: VIG Re Secures Singapore Regulatory Approval to Advance Asia-Pacific Expansion
item.description: VIG Re has received regulatory approval from the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) to establish a Representative Office in Singapore, marking a key milestone in the reinsurer’s long-term expansion strategy across Asia-Pacific. Strategic Hub for Asia-Pacific The Singapore Representative Office will serve as a liaison and coordination hub between VIG Re’s headquarters and its clients, business partners and stakeholders across the Asia-Pacific region. Experienced Regional Leadership The Singapore office will be led by  Wilfrid Goh , who has been appointed  Chief Representative, Asia Pacific .

item.title: Liberty Mutual Investments commits $320 million to restaurant platform inKind
item.description: Liberty Mutual Investments , the investment firm for Liberty MutualLiberty Mutual , has committed more than $320 million to restaurant commerce platform inKind, serving as both senior and mezzanine lender. Liberty Mutual Investments understands that this is not just about financing transactions—it is about backing entrepreneurs, creating jobs, and helping the places where communities gather continue to thrive.” – Johann Moonesinghe, CEO and co-founder of inKind . This transaction reflects our conviction in businesses that expand economic opportunity through innovative solutions, and our long-term capital and ability to invest across capital structures position us to be a strategic partner as inKind continues to scale its impact.” – John Kim, Head of Alternative Credit at Liberty Mutual Investments.

item.title: ISC launches workers’ compensation platform
item.description: Multi-line program administrator and MGA Integrated Specialty Coverages announced the launch of its workers’ compensation platform with BusinessFirst Insurance, a new brand that builds on RetailFirst Insurance Group, comprised of RetailFirst Insurance Company and BusinessFirst Insurance Company. Powered by ISC, BusinessFirst combines decades of workers’ compensation experience with “modern technology, data-driven decision-making, and industry-leading claims administration and analytics capabilities.” A-rated coverage is supported through BusinessFirst Insurance Company and RetailFirst Insurance Company. “BusinessFirst brings best-in-class coverage, focus, expertise, and consistency to Workers’ Compensation.

item.title: AIG appoints Nancy Bewlay as Global Chief Underwriting Officer
item.description: AIG announced that Nancy Bewlay has been named Executive Vice President, Global Chief Underwriting Officer, effective September 8, 2026. With more than 30 years’ experience and widely recognized across the industry for her underwriting expertise and leadership, Bewlay joins AIG from AXA Group, where she served as Group Chief Underwriting & Pricing Officer and a member of the Group Management Committee since 2023. “Nancy Bewlay is a deeply technical and experienced insurance executive with a distinguished track record of advancing underwriting excellence and building high-performing global underwriting teams.

item.title: «Somos una compañía integral de seguros y servicios»
item.description: El lanzamiento de esta nueva imagen marca un punto de inflexión en la evolución de una entidad que reivindica su condición de compañía “integral” de seguros y servicios, con su promesa intacta ‘Always Ready.

item.title: Camp Mystic Seeks Bankruptcy to Settle Texas Flood Wrongful Death Claims
item.description: Camp Mystic, the Texas girls camp where 28 people were killed in flash floods last summer, will use its bankruptcy case to resolve wrongful death claims filed against the family-owned company. Related: Camp Mystic, Where Texas Floods Killed 28, Files Bankruptcy “The testimony outside of this court show no real debate that the debtors are liable for deaths. “We acknowledge the pain and suffering and anguish,” caused by the deaths, Martin Sosland, a lawyer for Camp Mystic, said during a bankruptcy court hearing on Tuesday.

item.title: Tesla Settles Lawsuit Over Deadly Crash Involving Full Self-Driving
item.description: Read More: A Fatal Tesla Crash Shows the Limits of Full Self-Driving Attorney Dustin Birch, who represents Story’s daughter, said in a phone interview that the case recently settled and “my client is happy to put this behind her.” Terms of the settlement weren’t disclosed, and an attorney for Tesla didn’t respond to requests for comment. Moments later, she was struck at high speed by a Tesla Model Y SUV using the company’s so-called Full Self-Driving system. has quietly resolved a lawsuit stemming from a fatal 2023 crash that precipitated a defect investigation into the carmaker’s automated-driving technology.

item.title: Tune Protect Malaysia appoints Louise Lee as CEO
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Great American Insurance bolsters specialty underwriting team in Singapore
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Pricing is converging towards technical adequacy at 1.7, not overshooting it: Gallagher Re
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Statutory panel chair appointments confirmed for FCA listing authority and practitioner panels
item.description: The FCA has announced Kirsty Cooper will take up the role as Chair of the Listing Authority Advisory Panel (LAAP). Mandy has worked tirelessly supporting the FCA’s ambitious programme of innovation and reform of the market.' Kirsty Cooper, Non-Executive Director, Scottish Widows Group and Aon UK, said: 'I am delighted to be joining the Listing Authority Advisory Panel as Chair and look forward to working with the Panel members to deliver on our priorities. Clare Woodman and Matt Hammerstein have been reappointed as Chair of the FCA Markets Practitioner Panel and Chair of the FCA Practitioner Panel.

item.title: London-Based Tapestry VC Closes On $80M Third Fund To Invest In Repeat European Founders
item.description: London-based Tapestry VC has closed an $80 million third fund to double down on what it believes is one of Europe’s biggest long-term advantages: repeat founders. From its new fund, Tapestry plans to invest in a similar number of companies as it did with prior funds: Around 30 companies at pre-seed or seed. The firm says entrepreneurs starting their second or third companies have created more than $2 trillion in enterprise value across Europe, and expects the coming wave of AI exits to produce another generation of experienced founders.

item.title: Property cat rates fall 15-20% in LatAm at renewals, parametrics gain traction: Howden Re
item.description: At the July 1st 2026 reinsurance renewals property catastrophe excess of loss programmes in Latin America (LatAm) saw rate reductions fall in the range of 15% to 20%, while parametric products gained significant traction across the market, reports broker Howden Re. Howden Re also outlined two distinct emerging themes that were present at the LatAm renewals. At the same time, Howden Re observed that parametric products are gaining traction within the LatAm market, moving from a niche consideration to a more broadly evaluated alternative and supplemental coverage option.

item.title: Appetite for reinsurance brings moment for creativity. NA cat rates 20-25%+ down: Gallagher Re
item.description: The appetite of reinsurance capital to deploy has brought around a moment for creativity, broker Gallagher Re has said. In its First View report for the July 1 reinsurance renewals, broker Gallagher Re paints a picture of a market with abundant capital and an inexorable appetite to deploy it. Demand for reinsurance opportunities outstripped demand for protection at the July renewals, resulting in softening prices and North American property catastrophe rates tumbling 20% to 25% or more in some cases.

item.title: Cat bond market shows high bars are set to be broken, as records fall again in H1 2026: Report
item.description: Catastrophe bond market momentum accelerated through the second-quarter of 2026 and has broken many records, with more than $11.3 billion of new risk capital analysed and tracked in the period, making Q2 2026 the biggest single quarter in the history of the cat bond market and driving H1 to set another high bar, Artemis’ new quarterly catastrophe bond market report shows . The Artemis Q2 2026 catastrophe bond and related insurance-linked securities (ILS) market report examines a period that has set a new high bar for issuance. The Artemis Q2 2026 Catastrophe Bond and related insurance-linked securities (ILS) Market Report, available to download now, examines the cat bond and related ILS risk capital issued in the record second-quarter and key trends from the period, plus a review of the first-half.

item.title: Hippo broadens Accelerant partnership with expanded fronting carrier agreement
item.description: As part of the agreement, effective from 1 July 2026, with the full programme launching on 1 October 2026, Hippo Holdings will become the fronting carrier for more than $500 million in annual gross written premiums across Accelerant’s US portfolio during 2027. Hippo Holdings Inc, a US property and casualty insurance company that combines insurance with technology-driven risk management, has entered into an expanded partnership with Accelerant, a provider of a data-led risk exchange platform for the specialty insurance sector. Commenting on the expanded collaboration, Jeff Radke, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Accelerant, added: “The partnership with Hippo strengthens the Accelerant Risk Exchange for our Members and the insureds.

item.title: Kim Plant joins Miller as Head of North American Property
item.description: Independent specialist re/insurance broker Miller has appointed Kim Plant as Head of North American Property, where he will lead the firm’s North American Property proposition from its London and Bermuda offices. Plant joins Miller from Amwins, where he served as a Director in its North American Property Division from 2020, and reportedly holds extensive relationships across the North American Property market in both the US and Canada. “With his extensive market expertise and long-standing relationships, Kim is perfectly positioned to lead our Property proposition in the region as we continue to respond to increasing demand for our services.” Plant added, “It’s a pleasure to be joining Miller at this exciting time of growth for its North American Property Business.

item.title: Compre appoints Graham Taylor as MD of Underwriting
item.description: Compre Group Holdings Limited (Compre), a Bermuda-domiciled international specialty reinsurance group, has announced the appointment of Graham Taylor as managing director of underwriting, effective immediately. Graham’s extensive experience and commercial acumen will further strengthen our ability to deliver high-quality solutions for our clients.” Commenting on his appointment, Taylor said: “I’m extremely pleased to join Compre, with its long history of innovation and at such an exciting time in company’s development. In the role, Taylor will report to Chief Underwriting Officer Rachel Bardon and will play a key part in leading the group’s retrospective reinsurance activities, overseeing the execution of new transactions across its portfolio.
